headline: Penang Mangrove Loss Reaches 40% Since 1990
context: A 2025 USM remote sensing study found Penang has lost 40% of its mangrove cover since 1990, primarily to reclamation projects including the Penang South Islands development.

cards.big: Penang South Islands will create 4,500 acres of new land.
cards.sub: It will also destroy 800 acres of mangrove that provides RM120M annually in flood protection services.

cards.big: Mangroves absorb 3-5 times more carbon than inland forests.
cards.sub: Penang's remaining 1,200 hectares sequester an estimated 48,000 tonnes of CO2 annually.

cards.big: The EIA was approved despite 4,200 public objections.
cards.sub: DOE approved the assessment within 90 days — the minimum statutory period.

cards.big: Fishermen in Teluk Kumbar report 35% catch decline since reclamation began.
cards.sub: No compensation framework exists for gradual livelihood loss from habitat destruction.

cards.big: Reclamation creates land but destroys the ecosystems that protect the land behind it.
Coastal development that ignores ecosystem services is borrowing from nature without a repayment plan. The bill arrives during the next storm surge.
